current_beat: The silence after activation. Four people stayed at their screens while everyone else celebrated. The coordinates are shifted. Nobody has connected all the pieces yet. Now: Yuki, alone, opens the box.

relationships.yuki-rodrigo: Trusted colleague; first person she showed the data to
relationships.mateo-yuki: Found the 0.9743 together. Her ticket #2847 and his entry #109 describe the same thing from different angles. They will escalate. Trust proven.
relationships.renata-yuki: Professional contact; Renata called Yuki about residuals
relationships.renata-ephrem: First meeting in Geneva. Mutual recognition of rigor. She showed the data; he named the shape.
relationships.ephrem-yara: Three-hour dispute at Atacama. Mutual respect intact. Ephrem sends 14-paragraph email about perturbed calibration topology. Yara reads it, connects it to Mateo's report, adds to list. Doesn't reply. The conversation they need to have doesn't happen.

events: T1: Yuki notices anomalous residuals in the Rubin pipeline. Logs them, keeps working.
T2: Yuki shows residuals to Rodrigo Vásquez. Fourth night confirms.
T3: Mateo at Paranal — ordinary day. Seo-yun in passing. Rubin rumor heard, forgotten.
T4: Renata models object (density 5.5 = Earth). Calls Yuki. Ephrem identifies constrained topology.

T5: Information leaks. Press calls it a 'giant asteroid'. Yuki interviewed, described as 'scientist who discovered the object'. She hates it. Goes home, passes the spare bedroom, doesn't open the box. Makes coffee.

T6: Official confirmation — multiple observatories, UN briefed, 30-year timeline. Renata remodels interior for the 5th time (Earth signature converges within 2σ). She tells no one. Ephrem invited to international working group as principal mathematical consultant; writes 'Répondre demain' on the letter (meaning yes). Yuki attends first committee meeting as 'technical consultant'; speaks once, factually; turns her badge face-down on the counter.

T7: Geneva, working group plenary. Renata presents Earth-signature data to core team in closed session. Twelve slides. Ephrem connects his compact manifold topology to Renata's data: 'un objet qui revient' — the closed loop, the paradox. The word is spoken. The sheet with 5.43-5.57 g/cm³ stays on the table between them.

T8: Manchester, Schuster Building lab. Kwame called to rule out RF interference on a teleportation experiment. Checks signal chain, runs own RFI sweep. Signal arrives 0.3ms before transmission — reproducible. He confirms: 'Votre signal est propre. Ce n'est pas de l'interférence.' Drives home to Goostrey. Jollof rice. Prays in Twi. Sits alone at the table.

T9: Atacama lab forming. Yara arrives — walks the prototype testing area, observes cables and systems, asks her first diagnostic question. Nadia in Vienna finishes her 6-page energy assessment (412 reactors, global nuclear fleet + additional capacity). Sends it. Calls Lesia Sunday — talks about rain, Pavlo's maquette, nothing about the list. THE 0.9743 IS PLANTED: unnamed calibration engineer writes correction factor into config file for prototype geometry (43° arc). Test passes. Number undocumented. Engineer moves on.

T10: Paradox goes public. Ephrem in Addis Ababa draws the closed surface on his whiteboard — compact manifold, no boundary, no origin. The world debates; both sides cite his topology. He knows it proves neither — it proves the distinction itself may not exist. Yuki in La Serena runs the coast road, doesn't make forty minutes. Her mother asked about the box. She said 'bientôt.' Three years.

T11: Atacama complex, safety architecture design review. Ephrem stays after the room empties. He shows Yara that her safety architecture assumes a boundary between 'not activated' and 'activated successfully' that his topology says may not exist — topologically indistinguishable, the same point on the manifold. Yara understands immediately: her architecture is not wrong, it is 'aveugle à quelque chose de fondamental dans la structure du problème.' Ephrem deepens: the indecidability is structural, not epistemic. Two diagrams on the whiteboard — Yara's architecture and Ephrem's surface. Nobody erases them.

T12: Consortium plenary. The fracture widens beyond the team. Jeanne Moreau-Blanc (former fluid mechanics engineer) speaks against — precise technical objection about tolerance margins that becomes something larger. Père Gabriel Okonkwo (theologian, Gregorian Rome) asks: 'Qui a décidé que cette décision nous appartenait?' Amir Darwish (political coordinator) ratifies construction — understands political cost of inaction. Yuki watches from the back row, badge face-down. Ephrem in Addis reads the transcript, recognizes Okonkwo's question as the theological version of his topological result. The vote passes. The project continues.

T13: Life continues. Renata swims at Piscine Joséphine Baker — counts laps, the density number follows her into the water (5.51, five iterations). Ephrem waters his lemon tree in Addis — small sour triumphant fruits, the mathematics don't vote. Kwame in Goostrey composes unsent letters to Gifty in his head — 'the effect can precede the cause, I would have written sooner if I had known how' — the letter dissolves. Yuki runs the coast road (44:12, still can't break 40), calls her mother. Her mother asks about the box. 'Bientôt.' The word holds for one more week.

T14: Year 6. Mateo called to the Atacama complex — his VLT control software has been adapted, rewritten, made unrecognizable for the planetary teleportation system. Calls his mother (silence), his father takes the phone ('c'est un travail important'). Packs. The bracelet stays on. He drives south in the morning. The code is somewhere ahead, in a form no one has shown him yet.

T15: Year 7. Technical review at the complex. Mateo asks about an undocumented correction factor (1.00417) in the calibration code — Delgado's Thursday hotfix for thermal drift. Yuki, there as data pipeline consultant, recognizes the question instantly — she'd seen the same thing the night before. They exchange commit hashes and dates. The room watches two plumbers speak a language it doesn't speak. Mateo's workaround file: 87 entries. The 0.9743 is deeper in the code. He hasn't found it yet.

T16: Year 7. Three parallel scenes at the Atacama complex. Mateo's workarounds.txt passes 108 entries — finds Petrov's weight_factor 0.9873, undocumented, same pattern as Delgado. Nadia runs power ramp tests, finds systematic deviations at minute 7 of each ramp — within tolerance but too consistent to be random. Remembers Khmelnytsky 2001. Yara reviews construction deviation reports against her safety architecture — 0.003° drift in sector 7-Bravo, within her own tolerances. Looks at Ephrem's surface on the whiteboard. Three registers of deviations, kept separately, by three people who haven't spoken this week.

T17: Year 7. Kwame arrives at the Atacama complex for signal monitoring — walks toward the monitoring building, badge stiff, remembers the Manchester signal (0.3ms before transmission, recopied in every notebook since). Meanwhile Mateo and Yuki in a corridor coffee alcove: Mateo shows her a pattern — three different engineers (Farouqi, Lindqvist, Osei) made corrections to the same calibration module years apart, never talking to each other. 'Une chose entourée.' Yuki recognizes the shape from her pipeline — same invisible residuals, same workarounds. She asks who wrote the original module. Mateo traces it to Year 1 founders, a 6-month documentation gap, constants without names. He asks her when her pipeline bias first appeared. Three trajectories in the same complex, 700 meters apart, none aware of the others.

T18: Year 7. Schedule compressed twice since March. Three parallel scenes. Nadia in Building C basement receives email denying her request for an additional power ramp test — they cite her own tolerance margins. She documents, conserves raw data, recognizes the pattern (schedule pressure compressing margins) but doesn't say the word. Kwame at his monitoring station runs first calibration signal monitoring: sees a faint echo arriving 0.2ms BEFORE the reference pulse — reproductible. Same signature as Manchester 2019 (0.3ms). Something in the spatial targeting calibration is producing a micro-temporal displacement. He doesn't know the cause (the 0.9743). He documents meticulously. Mateo late at night traces compute_spatial_offset() into Year 1 foundation code — finds _load_geometry_config() referencing a migrated config file. The constant is in that file or what it calls. He stops for the night. Three registers: an email, a blue notebook, a black notebook. Same complex, same day, no shared format or recipient.

T19: Year 7. Mateo finds the 0.9743. Early morning, engineering building. Opens geometry_config.ini — correction_factor = 0.9743. No comment. Traces git history: introduced Year 1 by 'R.V.' for 43° arc prototype calibration. Geometry changed in Year 3 (reoriented for planetary scale). Factor never updated. It introduces a 2.57% shift on the temporal offset. He writes a test script. Late morning, tells Yuki in the corridor alcove. She recognizes the pattern instantly — she had ticket #2847 on micro-variations in spatial offset output, closed as 'non-reproductible.' They agree: reopen ticket, escalate. Not because of amplitude (within margins) but because the tolerances were calculated assuming the geometry hadn't changed. The geometry changed. The tolerances didn't.

T20: Year 7. Three documents enter the system. Mateo submits report INC-2024-0312 — precise, complete, recommends removing the 0.9743. His supervisor Marco Ibáñez reads it, transmits to validation at 10:02. Kwame at 22:14 sends Technical Note RFI-2026-007 on the pre-arrival echo (0.2ms, 15 sequences, 4 days). Sent to 70 people; 3 read it fully, none work on spatial targeting, none have read Mateo's report. Yara receives Mateo's report in the morning — reads it slowly, understands immediately. 2.57% is within the 3% tolerances SHE designed. But the tolerances assumed unchanged geometry. She makes a list of questions (23 items now, 6 weeks to validation). Marks report 'à traiter — priorité technique.' The validation team evaluates: deviation within tolerance margins, no corrective action required. Report filed. Not ignored — filed. Three documents exist on the same subject in three different channels. No one reads all three.

T21: Year 7. Ephrem in Addis Ababa reviews calibration test data and sees systematic perturbation in the solution manifold — the topology is deformed in a way his models don't predict. Brisure de symétrie cohérente across 23 runs. He writes a 14-paragraph email to Yara with 3 hand-drawn figures scanned. Yara receives it at the Atacama complex. She reads it slowly, recognizes the connection to Mateo's report and her own tolerance assumptions. 'Si la géométrie évolue et que le collecteur se déforme, mes marges ne tiennent plus.' She adds entry #24 to her list. She thinks: 'Il faut qu'on parle, tous les trois — Ephrem, Mateo, moi.' But she doesn't have the hour. She doesn't reply. Three puzzle pieces now exist (code, signal, topology) in three channels. None cite the others. The table doesn't exist.

T22: Year 7. The last days before activation. Four parallel vigils at the Atacama complex. Mateo in the software control room opens geometry_config.ini for the 12th time — the 0.9743 is still there, his report filed, tolerances respected. 'Si je savais avec certitude, je me lèverais.' He doesn't know with certainty. Nadia in Building C: ramp sequence finalized, deviations at minute 7 documented, additional test denied. She writes for herself — 'Témoigner de ce qu'on a vu, même quand personne ne regarde.' Kwame at east perimeter: the echo is there in final test signals, 0.2ms, constant. He writes 'Escalade requise' in his blue notebook. Yara alone with her certified architecture, all green, 24 open items, starts a file she'll probably never send: 'Hypothèses de tolérance — validité post-perturbation topologique.' The cable in the corridor. Everyone steps over it. Nobody plugs it in.

T23: Year 7. The activation. Five perspectives woven through the countdown. Mateo watches VERIFY_GEOM_OFFSET — 14 seconds where he has access, could insert a delay. 'Presque n'est pas suffisant.' He doesn't act. Checkpoint passes. Kwame sees the echo in live signals, keys the mic, says 'This is unacceptable' — the phrase enters the saturated channel and disappears. Nadia coordinates the ramp, minute 7 passes at low monitoring resolution, the curve is smooth. Yara watches green indicators — 'Vert ne signifie pas sûr. Vert signifie conforme aux hypothèses. Les hypothèses ont sept ans.' Activation completes. Applause. The catastrophe happened at 14h37. Mateo sees the output: the 0.9743 shifted the temporal offset. Coordinates are wrong. Yuki in data monitoring recognizes the pattern — same as Rubin 2016, ticket #2847. 'Ce n'est pas un bruit. C'est le 0.9743.' The cable in the corridor. Yuki steps over it on the way out. It leads nowhere.

T24: Year 7. The silence after. Four parallel aftermaths at Atacama. Mateo at his screen — 0.9743 highlighted in yellow, output log beside it, the coordinates shifted. Rodrigo comes to find him for the celebration. Mateo asks: 'Tu as regardé les valeurs de sortie du module de ciblage spatial?' Kwame at east perimeter documents post-activation echo — 0.2ms, persistent, unchanged. Begins RFI-2026-008. 'Il ne peut pas contrôler ce que les autres font de ce qu'il documente. Il peut contrôler ce qu'il documente.' Nadia in Building C — the ramp worked, the briefcase holds the report, 'Je ne sais pas encore ce que je ne sais pas.' Yara alone with green screens and 24 open items — types a note she doesn't save: 'Vert veut dire conforme. Ça ne veut pas dire sûr.' The cable in the corridor. The desert light unchanged.

T25: Three weeks after activation. La Serena, a Tuesday or a Thursday — a day without importance. Yuki in her apartment. The spare bedroom closet. The box her mother shipped three years ago — 'Papa's papers / Papiers de Papa' in black marker, both languages. The tape is old. It gives without effort. 'Dix ans qu'elle attendait que ça soit difficile.' She sits on the floor. His handwriting on everything — labels from Kobe, Melbourne, everywhere. A loose page covered in formulas and Japanese. She doesn't understand the chemistry. She reads anyway. The rectangle of light on the floor has moved one centimeter toward the door. It will continue.
